results.title: Policy enforcement for Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex
results.content: | |  |  | Show HN: Policy enforcement for Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex (kastra.ai) | | 13 points by carlosjimenez1 9 days ago | hide | past | favorite | 5 comments | | Show HN: Runtime authorization for Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex Hi HN, Fernando and I built Kastra. Kastra intercepts AI agent tool calls and evaluates them against deterministic policies before they execute. This is aimed at developers using coding agents like Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and OpenClaw.  We built Kastra after one of our Cursor agents almost executed DELETE FROM customers WHERE status='test' against a production database. We caught it before it ran, but it made us realize that nothing in our stack actually decided what the agent was allowed to do. What mattered for us wasn't the mistake; it was realizing [...] and Recon scan are free to use for developers.  If you often use Claude, Codex, Openclaw, and Cursor, Kastra can run a scan command on which risky actions your agents have already taken and automatically build rules to avoid them from happening again. Recon is a feature of Kastra that scans your local agent history. In order to run this scan, execute the commands below in your coding agent.  brew install kastra-labs/tap/kastra-edge  kastra-edge scan  The scan reads your local agent session history, and it shows all the risky actions your agent has already taken before, the secrets written to tracked files, production databases touched, force pushes, curl-to-shell, and more. This runs on your machine, and secrets never leave. In our own use cases, we kept finding things we'd forgotten or [...] so we can see what the most common patterns are and adjust policy packs for our users based on your feedback.  Documentation:   Download for MacOS Kastra Edge:   Check Kastra in action today:  | |  |     |  |  |  --- | | |  |  | josefritzishere 8 days ago  | next (javascript:void(0))   The fact that AI cannot do this on it's own is the most concerning thing of all.  reply | | | |  |  | fervinta 8 days ago  | parent | next (javascript:void(0))   Agreed. But AI is still probabilistic. We see them as a strong signal but not the final authority. We believe that authorization should stay outside the model so it is deterministic and auditable. Similar to how AWS IAM works.  reply | | | |  |  | pizzafeelsright 9 days ago  | prev | next (javascript:void(0))   after one of our Cursor agents almost

results.title: OpenClaw Claude Auth currently broken? Anyone else seeing this?
results.content:
a workaround or a fix, please share. Environment: Ubuntu server OpenClaw v2026.3.x Installed via official installer Gateway running with dashboard Curious if this is a bug or a deprecated feature . [...] I was trying to enable Claude authentication in OpenClaw using the CLI, but it looks like the Anthropic auth flow isn’t working anymore. What I tried: Run Claude auth login openclaw models auth login --provider anthropic OpenClaw returned: Error: No provider plugins found. Install one via 'openclaw plugins install'. Tried installing the plugin: openclaw plugins install anthropic But the installation failed with: package.json missing openclaw.extensions So the plugin cannot be installed and Claude login authentication does not work . For now the only working approach seems to be: Use an Anthropic API key in the environment config Has anyone managed to get Claude auth login working in recent OpenClaw versions (2026.x) ? If you have a workaround or a fix, please share. Environment: Ubuntu [...] # OpenClaw Claude Auth currently broken? Anyone else seeing this?

```
# clawo-mcp is now on PATH #
```

Register `clawo-mcp` with any MCP-compatible host: Hermes Agent, Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline, Continue, Zed, Windsurf, Goose, and others. Per-host stdio-config snippets and the `CLAWO_MCP_TOOLS` allowlist for tight tool budgets are in `mcp.md`.

## Engine Compatibility

| Engine | CLI | Tested Version |
 --- 
| Claude Code | `claude` | 2.1.217 |
| Codex | `codex` | 0.145.0 |
| Antigravity | `agy` | 1.1.5 |
| Cursor Agent | `agent` | 2026.07.20-8cc9c0b |
| OpenCode | `opencode` | 1.18.4 |
| Custom CLI | any | — |

Any coding CLI that runs as a subprocess can be wired up as a custom engine — see `multi-engine.md`.

results.title: Claude KILLED OpenClaw — Here's How to Fix It in 60 Seconds
results.content: Claude code is basically Anthropic changed their policy. Claude code subscription tokens can no longer be used by third-party tools. So, Open Claude and everything else connected through that token gets blocked. So, before you would basically be able to use your Claude code subscription. You'd be paying a monthly fee for it and now well, only the official Claude code CLI can be used. So, basically if you want to use Claude with Open Claude, you can you would have to use the API key which is just way too expensive. So, if you see an error with Open Claude and you're trying to use the Claude code subscription, it's not a bug. It's real. Like Claude is blocking that. Now, what you'll see right now is this. So, authentication failed. Subscription tokens no longer accepted and basically let me [...] Subscription tokens no longer accepted and basically let me show you exactly how to fix this. So, what you're going to do is basically you're going to run Open Claude configure and then it's going to tell you what is going to give you the option to select a specific model. So, you can select between Anthropic, Open AI and then Chinese models, Gemini, etc. Now, what we're going to do here is we're going to select Open AI. So, Open AI has a Codex subscription that you can directly use which is very similar to Claude code. So, it's going to basically ask you to log in through the Open AI OAuth. You don't need a subscription for this. I actually use it without a subscription and it works pretty well. So, yeah, you just need to click on this link and then you need to authorize authorize it and [...] this link and then you need to authorize authorize it and then approve the connection and then you'll be in. Then you need to restart your Open Claude. By the way, all of these commands will be in the description, so just check that out. So, yeah, you'll need to restart your gateway. This is where many people get it wrong. They forget to restart the gateway and then they just stuck thinking that they made a change, but actually the change only happens after you restart your Open Claude gateway. Then you can just confirm that it's working by saying Open Claude model status and essentially it will just tell you that it's connected to Codex and you will also be able to see the fallbacks if you have some. Now, just one thing to inform you guys as well, what we do what I do is basically we

results.title: OC Problems.. Im freaking out.. : r/openclaw
results.content:
- I told OC to increase timeout and that helped
- I’m running 05.28 and having this same issue. Only with codex (gpt-5.4). Local models and my other sub provider doesn’t have this issue.
- How are you separating your flows? Is everything being dumped into 1 chat channel or is each workflow in its own isolated chat thread?
- Maybe leave the coding part to an IDE with agent plugins, or cursor, Claude code, codex and such [...] ## Top Comments
- I was having this issue on 5.22 using a ROCm llama.cpp as my backend for local AI use. I since updated to 5.28 and swapped over to Codex Runtime since im not really fully utilizing my Codex subscription. This seemed to fix my issues and my agent no longer drops responses mid task. [...] - you can ask more in deep your agent why it happened..in my case I asked and she explained that during some specific tasks she is blocked and cannot answer me..It was a time ago...but I do thing it was a process called flush or something.. also you need to have in consideration that your way of using or is she has to access many skills can fill the context quickly and also if she might need to start a new session she can loss memory of the task. revisit first the way you are using it as you might need to optimize it.
- Change the agents Ask setting and the authority
- None
- Yep, I’ve become used to an occasional: Poke…
- I told OC to increase timeout and that helped
